# Coding Session -- Architecture Design

## 1. System Context

The coding-session feature adds an interactive supervisory layer between Brain's human operator and spawned OpenCode coding agents. It wires existing infrastructure (spawn, event bridge, stall detector, SSE registry) into a live feedback loop.

### C4 Level 1: System Context

```mermaid
graph TB
    User["Marcus (Solo Operator)"]
    Brain["Brain Platform"]
    OpenCode["OpenCode Agent Process"]
    GitRepo["Git Repository (Worktree)"]
    SurrealDB["SurrealDB"]

    User -- "assigns tasks, sends follow-up prompts,\nreviews diffs, accepts/rejects" --> Brain
    Brain -- "spawns, sends prompts,\nreceives event stream" --> OpenCode
    OpenCode -- "edits files in\nisolated worktree" --> GitRepo
    Brain -- "persists sessions,\nconversation logs" --> SurrealDB
    Brain -- "streams agent tokens,\nstatus, file changes via SSE" --> User
```

### C4 Level 2: Container Diagram

```mermaid
graph TB
    subgraph Client["Browser Client (Vanilla JS + React)"]
        AgentPanel["AgentSessionPanel"]
        AgentOutput["AgentSessionOutput"]
        PromptInput["FollowUpPromptInput"]
        ReviewPage["Review Page (Diff + Agent Log tabs)"]
        SSEHook["useAgentSession Hook"]
    end

    subgraph Server["Bun HTTP Server"]
        Routes["Orchestrator Routes"]
        Lifecycle["Session Lifecycle"]
        HandleReg["Handle Registry (in-memory)"]
        EventBridge["Event Bridge"]
        StallDetect["Stall Detector"]
        LogPersist["Conversation Log Persistence"]
        SSEReg["SSE Registry"]
    end

    subgraph External["External Processes"]
        OpenCode["OpenCode Server Process"]
    end

    subgraph Data["Data Layer"]
        SurrealDB["SurrealDB"]
    end

    AgentPanel -- "renders" --> AgentOutput
    AgentPanel -- "renders" --> PromptInput
    SSEHook -- "subscribes via EventSource" --> SSEReg
    PromptInput -- "POST /prompt" --> Routes
    ReviewPage -- "GET /review + GET /log" --> Routes

    Routes -- "delegates to" --> Lifecycle
    Lifecycle -- "stores/retrieves handles" --> HandleReg
    Lifecycle -- "for-await-of eventStream" --> OpenCode
    Lifecycle -- "pipes events through" --> EventBridge
    EventBridge -- "emits StreamEvents" --> SSEReg
    EventBridge -- "notifies" --> StallDetect
    EventBridge -- "appends log entries" --> LogPersist
    LogPersist -- "persists conversation log" --> SurrealDB
    Lifecycle -- "reads/writes session state" --> SurrealDB
    HandleReg -- "sendPrompt(text)" --> OpenCode
```

## 2. Component Architecture

### 2.1 Server Components

| Component | Location | Responsibility | Status |
|-----------|----------|---------------|--------|
| spawn-opencode | `orchestrator/spawn-opencode.ts` | Spawn OpenCode process, expose handle | **EXISTS** -- complete |
| event-bridge | `orchestrator/event-bridge.ts` | Transform OpenCode events to StreamEvents | **EXISTS** -- complete |
| stall-detector | `orchestrator/stall-detector.ts` | Monitor session activity, abort on stall | **EXISTS** -- complete |
| session-lifecycle | `orchestrator/session-lifecycle.ts` | Session CRUD, state machine, handle registry | **EXISTS** -- needs event iteration wiring |
| routes | `orchestrator/routes.ts` | HTTP route handlers | **EXISTS** -- needs prompt endpoint |
| sse-registry | `streaming/sse-registry.ts` | SSE stream management | **EXISTS** -- needs orchestrator wiring |
| conversation-log | `orchestrator/conversation-log.ts` | Persist/retrieve agent conversation log | **NEW** |

### 2.2 Client Components

| Component | Location | Responsibility | Status |
|-----------|----------|---------------|--------|
| AgentStatusSection | `components/graph/AgentStatusSection.tsx` | Status badge, assign button | **EXISTS** -- needs panel delegation |
| useAgentSession | `hooks/use-agent-session.ts` | SSE subscription, state reducer | **EXISTS** -- needs token accumulation |
| orchestrator-api | `graph/orchestrator-api.ts` | Client fetch wrappers | **EXISTS** -- needs sendPrompt + getLog |
| AgentSessionOutput | `components/graph/AgentSessionOutput.tsx` | Token stream display, auto-scroll | **NEW** |
| AgentSessionPanel | `components/graph/AgentSessionPanel.tsx` | Composite: output + prompt input | **NEW** |
| AgentLogView | `components/graph/AgentLogView.tsx` | Chronological conversation log for review | **NEW** |

### 2.3 Shared Contracts

| Type | Location | Status |
|------|----------|--------|
| AgentTokenEvent | `shared/contracts.ts` | **EXISTS** |
| AgentFileChangeEvent | `shared/contracts.ts` | **EXISTS** |
| AgentStatusEvent | `shared/contracts.ts` | **EXISTS** |
| AgentStallWarningEvent | `shared/contracts.ts` | **EXISTS** |
| AgentPromptEvent | `shared/contracts.ts` | **NEW** -- user prompt echoed in stream |
| ConversationLogEntry | `shared/contracts.ts` | **NEW** -- structured log entry for persistence |

## 3. Data Flow

### 3.1 Event Stream Flow (US-CS-003 + US-CS-001)

```
OpenCode Process
  |
  | AsyncIterable<OpencodeEvent>
  v
createOrchestratorSession (for-await-of loop, fire-and-forget)
  |
  | OpencodeEvent
  v
startEventBridge.handleEvent()
  |-- transformOpencodeEvent() -> StreamEvent
  |-- deps.emitEvent(streamId, streamEvent) -> SSE Registry
  |-- deps.updateLastEventAt(sessionId) -> SurrealDB
  |-- stallDetector.recordActivity()
  |-- appendLogEntry(sessionId, logEntry) -> SurrealDB
  |
  v
SSE Registry -> EventSource -> useAgentSession hook
  |
  | reduceAgentSessionEvent()
  v
AgentSessionOutput (renders accumulated tokens)
```

### 3.2 Follow-Up Prompt Flow (US-CS-002)

```
User types in PromptInput
  |
  | POST /api/orchestrator/:ws/sessions/:id/prompt { text }
  v
promptSession route handler
  |-- validate session exists and is active
  |-- look up OpenCodeHandle from handleRegistry
  |-- handle.sendPrompt(text) (fire-and-forget)
  |-- emit AgentPromptEvent to SSE stream (echo back to client)
  |-- appendLogEntry(sessionId, { type: "user_prompt", text })
  |
  v
Response: 202 Accepted
```

### 3.3 Review with Agent Log (US-CS-004)

```
User navigates to review page
  |
  | GET /api/orchestrator/:ws/sessions/:id/review (existing)
  | GET /api/orchestrator/:ws/sessions/:id/log (new)
  v
Review page renders two tabs:
  - Diff tab (existing)
  - Agent Log tab (new: chronological conversation trail)

Reject with Feedback:
  |
  | POST /api/orchestrator/:ws/sessions/:id/reject { feedback }
  v
rejectOrchestratorSession (existing)
  |-- sets status to "active"
  |-- handle.sendPrompt(feedback) via handleRegistry
  |-- appendLogEntry(sessionId, { type: "user_prompt", text: feedback })
```

## 4. Architecture Decisions

### 4.1 SSE Registry Wiring Gap

**Finding**: `wireOrchestratorRoutes` is called WITHOUT `sseRegistry` in `start-server.ts`. The SSE registry exists at `deps.sse` but is not passed to the orchestrator wiring.

**Resolution**: Pass `deps.sse` as `sseRegistry` to `wireOrchestratorRoutes`. Also register the stream in the SSE registry when creating a session (call `sseRegistry.registerMessage(streamId)` before spawning).

### 4.2 Orchestrator SSE Stream Route

**Finding**: No stream route is registered for orchestrator sessions. The chat system has `/api/chat/stream/:messageId` but the orchestrator's stream handler (created in `routes.ts` when `sseRegistry` is provided) has no registered URL path.

**Resolution**: Add route `/api/orchestrator/:workspaceId/sessions/:sessionId/stream` that delegates to the SSE registry's `handleStreamRequest(streamId)`.

### 4.3 Event Iteration Strategy

The `for-await-of` loop over `handle.eventStream` runs as a fire-and-forget background task after `createOrchestratorSession` returns. It must:
- Not block the HTTP response
- Catch errors and update session status
- Stop on terminal status events
- Clean up resources on completion

### 4.4 Token Accumulation in useAgentSession

The existing hook tracks `status`, `filesChanged`, `stallWarning` but does NOT accumulate `agent_token` events. The hook must be extended to accumulate tokens into a structured array and expose them to `AgentSessionOutput`.

Token accumulation model: **structured array** (not string concat). Each entry carries the token text and a timestamp, enabling the output component to render inline file-change notifications at the correct position in the stream.

### 4.5 Conversation Log Persistence

Server-side persistence (SurrealDB) chosen over client-side session storage. Rationale: log must survive page navigation, browser refresh, and be available on the review page. See ADR-005.

## 5. Integration Points

| Integration | Mechanism | Contract |
|-------------|-----------|----------|
| Server -> OpenCode | SDK client (HTTP) | `sendPrompt(text)`, `eventStream` |
| Server -> Client | SSE via `sse-registry` | `StreamEvent` union type |
| Client -> Server | HTTP REST | Orchestrator route handlers |
| Server -> SurrealDB | Surreal SDK | `agent_session` table + new `conversation_log` fields |
| Event Bridge -> SSE | `emitEvent(streamId, event)` | `StreamEvent` discriminated union |

## 6. Quality Attribute Strategies

| Attribute | Strategy |
|-----------|----------|
| **Responsiveness** | Fire-and-forget event iteration; 202 for prompt endpoint; SSE for real-time push |
| **Reliability** | Stall detector (existing); SSE reconnection (useAgentSession error handler); session state in DB survives client disconnects |
| **Maintainability** | Pure transform functions (event bridge); dependency injection (function signatures as ports); shared contracts |
| **Testability** | Pure core/effect shell pattern throughout; injectable clock/deps in stall detector; state reducer is pure |
| **Simplicity** | Reuse existing SSE registry, event bridge, stall detector; no new infrastructure components |

## 7. Known Limitations

- **Server restart loses handles**: In-memory `handleRegistry` is ephemeral. Active sessions become orphaned on restart. Documented limitation -- not addressed in this feature.
- **No SSE reconnection for orchestrator streams**: If EventSource drops, client shows "Connection lost" but does not auto-reconnect. The useAgentSession hook closes on error. This matches the existing chat SSE behavior.
- **Conversation log size**: Unbounded for now. Agent sessions are short-lived (minutes to hours). Cap at 10,000 entries if needed later.

# Coding Session -- Technology Stack

All technology choices are existing in the project. No new dependencies required.

## Runtime

| Component | Technology | Version | License | Rationale |
|-----------|-----------|---------|---------|-----------|
| Server runtime | Bun | existing | MIT | Already in use |
| Database | SurrealDB | v3.0 | BSL 1.1 | Already in use; graph model fits conversation log |
| Coding agent | OpenCode | existing | MIT | Already integrated via `@opencode-ai/sdk` |

## Server

| Component | Technology | Rationale |
|-----------|-----------|-----------|
| HTTP server | Bun.serve | Existing; custom route matching |
| SSE streaming | Custom `sse-registry.ts` | Existing; proven pattern from chat system |
| Event transform | Custom `event-bridge.ts` | Existing; pure transform functions |
| Process management | Node `child_process.spawn` | Existing; manages OpenCode server lifecycle |
| Session state | SurrealDB `agent_session` table | Existing; schema already has orchestrator fields |

## Client

| Component | Technology | Rationale |
|-----------|-----------|-----------|
| UI framework | React (vanilla, no meta-framework) | Existing |
| SSE consumption | Browser `EventSource` API | Existing; used by `useAgentSession` hook |
| State management | React hooks (`useState`, `useCallback`, `useRef`) | Existing pattern; no external state library |
| HTTP client | Browser `fetch` API | Existing; wrapped in `orchestrator-api.ts` |

## New Dependencies

**None.** This feature is entirely wiring existing components together plus adding UI components using existing patterns. No new npm packages, no new infrastructure.

## Alternatives Considered

| Decision | Chosen | Rejected | Why Rejected |
|----------|--------|----------|-------------|
| SSE library | Browser EventSource (existing) | Socket.io, ws | EventSource already working; no WebSocket features needed |
| State management | React hooks | Zustand, Redux | Solo dev, component-local state sufficient; workspace store already uses Zustand minimally |
| Log persistence | SurrealDB fields | Redis, SQLite sidecar | SurrealDB already running; no justification for additional data store |
| Diff rendering | Raw text (existing) | monaco-diff, diff2html | Review page already renders diffs; enhancement is adding log tab alongside |

# Coding Session -- Component Boundaries

## Server-Side Boundaries

### Orchestrator Domain (`app/src/server/orchestrator/`)

Owns: session lifecycle, agent process management, event forwarding, conversation log persistence.

```
orchestrator/
  session-lifecycle.ts    [MODIFY] -- add event iteration loop, prompt forwarding, log persistence calls
  routes.ts               [MODIFY] -- add prompt endpoint, log endpoint
  event-bridge.ts         [MODIFY] -- add log entry emission alongside SSE emission
  spawn-opencode.ts       [NO CHANGE] -- already complete
  stall-detector.ts       [NO CHANGE] -- already complete
  conversation-log.ts     [NEW] -- conversation log CRUD (SurrealDB queries)
  types.ts                [MODIFY] -- add ConversationLogEntry type if needed
```

### Streaming Domain (`app/src/server/streaming/`)

Owns: SSE stream lifecycle, event delivery to clients.

```
streaming/
  sse-registry.ts         [NO CHANGE] -- already supports orchestrator event types
```

### Runtime Wiring (`app/src/server/runtime/`)

Owns: dependency injection, route registration.

```
runtime/
  start-server.ts         [MODIFY] -- pass sseRegistry to orchestrator wiring, add stream + prompt + log routes
```

### Shared Contracts (`app/src/shared/`)

Owns: types shared between server and client.

```
shared/
  contracts.ts            [MODIFY] -- add AgentPromptEvent, ConversationLogEntry
```

## Client-Side Boundaries

### Agent Session UI (`app/src/client/components/graph/`)

Owns: agent session visualization, interaction controls.

```
components/graph/
  AgentStatusSection.tsx  [MODIFY] -- delegate to AgentSessionPanel when session active
  AgentSessionPanel.tsx   [NEW] -- composite: output + prompt input + abort button
  AgentSessionOutput.tsx  [NEW] -- token stream display with auto-scroll
  AgentLogView.tsx        [NEW] -- chronological conversation log for review page
```

### Hooks (`app/src/client/hooks/`)

Owns: SSE subscription, state management.

```
hooks/
  use-agent-session.ts    [MODIFY] -- accumulate tokens + file changes into structured entries
```

### API Layer (`app/src/client/graph/`)

Owns: typed fetch wrappers.

```
graph/
  orchestrator-api.ts     [MODIFY] -- add sendPrompt(), getConversationLog()
```

## Boundary Rules

1. **Orchestrator domain does NOT import from chat domain.** SSE registry is the shared integration point, injected via dependency.
2. **Event bridge is the ONLY component that transforms OpenCode events.** All consumers receive `StreamEvent` types.
3. **Handle registry is internal to session-lifecycle.** Routes access handles indirectly through lifecycle functions.
4. **Conversation log queries are isolated in conversation-log.ts.** Session-lifecycle calls log functions but does not construct queries directly.
5. **Client components consume `useAgentSession` hook state.** They do NOT directly interact with EventSource or SSE.

## File Change Summary

|----------|-------------------|-----------|-------|
| Server | 4 | 1 | 5 |
| Client | 3 | 3 | 6 |
| Shared | 1 | 0 | 1 |
| Runtime | 1 | 0 | 1 |
| **Total** | **9** | **4** | **13** |

Step ratio check: 6 steps / 13 production files = 0.46 (well under 2.5 threshold).

# Coding Session -- Data Models

## Existing Schema (No Changes Required)

The `agent_session` table already has all fields needed for session lifecycle:

```
agent_session (SCHEMAFULL)
  agent: string
  started_at: datetime
  ended_at: option<datetime>
  workspace: record<workspace>
  task_id: option<record<task>>
  orchestrator_status: option<string>  -- "spawning" | "active" | "idle" | "completed" | "aborted" | "error"
  worktree_branch: option<string>
  worktree_path: option<string>
  stream_id: option<string>
  opencode_session_id: option<string>
  last_event_at: option<datetime>
  last_feedback: option<string>
  error_message: option<string>
  files_changed: option<array<object>>
  ...
```

## New Schema: Conversation Log

New field on `agent_session` to store the conversation log inline. Simpler than a separate table -- each session has exactly one log, entries are always accessed together, and sessions are short-lived.

### Schema Migration

```
DEFINE FIELD conversation_log ON agent_session TYPE option<array<object>>;
DEFINE FIELD conversation_log[*].entry_type ON agent_session TYPE string
  ASSERT $value IN ["agent_text", "file_change", "user_prompt", "status_change", "stall_warning"];
DEFINE FIELD conversation_log[*].timestamp ON agent_session TYPE datetime;
DEFINE FIELD conversation_log[*].content ON agent_session TYPE option<string>;
DEFINE FIELD conversation_log[*].file ON agent_session TYPE option<string>;
DEFINE FIELD conversation_log[*].change_type ON agent_session TYPE option<string>;
DEFINE FIELD conversation_log[*].status ON agent_session TYPE option<string>;
```

### Log Entry Types

| entry_type | content | file | change_type | status | When |
|-----------|---------|------|-------------|--------|------|
| `agent_text` | Token text (batched) | - | - | - | On `agent_token` events (batched per message turn) |
| `file_change` | - | File path | created/modified/deleted | - | On `agent_file_change` event |
| `user_prompt` | Prompt text | - | - | - | On POST /prompt or reject-with-feedback |
| `status_change` | - | - | - | active/idle/completed/error/aborted | On `agent_status` event |
| `stall_warning` | Warning message | - | - | - | On stall detection |

### Token Batching Strategy

`agent_token` events arrive as individual tokens (words/fragments). Appending each as a separate log entry would create thousands of entries. Instead:

- Accumulate tokens in memory during a message turn
- Flush as a single `agent_text` log entry when:
  - A `status_change` to `idle` occurs (turn complete)
  - A `user_prompt` is received (new turn boundary)
  - A configurable flush interval elapses (30s safety net)

This keeps the log at ~10-50 entries per session instead of thousands.

## Shared Contract Types

### AgentPromptEvent (new StreamEvent variant)

```
type: "agent_prompt"
sessionId: string
text: string
```

Emitted to SSE when a user sends a follow-up prompt. Allows the client to render user messages inline in the output stream without polling.

### ConversationLogEntry (new shared type)

```
entryType: "agent_text" | "file_change" | "user_prompt" | "status_change" | "stall_warning"
timestamp: string (ISO)
content?: string
file?: string
changeType?: string
status?: string
```

Used in the GET /log response and by the AgentLogView component.

## Client-Side State

### Extended AgentSessionState

The `useAgentSession` hook state adds:

- `tokens: Array<{ text: string; timestamp: string }>` -- accumulated token stream entries
- `fileChanges: Array<{ file: string; changeType: string; timestamp: string }>` -- inline file change notifications
- `userPrompts: Array<{ text: string; timestamp: string }>` -- echoed user prompts

These are interleaved by timestamp in the AgentSessionOutput component for chronological display.

## API Contracts

### POST /api/orchestrator/:ws/sessions/:id/prompt

Request: `{ text: string }`
Response: `202 Accepted` (empty body)
Errors: 404 (session not found), 409 (terminal status)

### GET /api/orchestrator/:ws/sessions/:id/log

Response: `{ entries: ConversationLogEntry[] }`
Errors: 404 (session not found)

Returns the persisted conversation log for the review page. The live session uses SSE events directly; this endpoint is for after-the-fact review.

input.content:
# ADR-005: Conversation Log Persistence Strategy

## Status
Accepted

## Context
The coding-session feature requires a chronological conversation log (agent output + user prompts + file changes) for two purposes: (1) live display during an active session, (2) review display after the agent finishes. The log must survive page navigation and browser refresh.

Team: solo developer. Timeline: 7-10 days. Database: SurrealDB already running.

## Decision
Persist conversation log entries server-side as an inline array field (`conversation_log`) on the existing `agent_session` SurrealDB table. Batch `agent_token` events in memory and flush as single `agent_text` entries at turn boundaries to keep the array at ~10-50 entries per session.

Live sessions use SSE events for real-time display. The persisted log is read via GET endpoint for the review page (Agent Log tab).

## Alternatives Considered

### Alternative 1: Client-side session storage
- What: Store log entries in browser sessionStorage/localStorage, no server persistence
- Expected Impact: Solves ~60% (live display works, review works only in same tab)
- Why Insufficient: Log lost on navigation, refresh, or tab close. Review page may open in different context (e.g., from governance feed link). Multi-device access impossible. Violates J3 requirement (review with full context).

### Alternative 2: Separate `conversation_log_entry` table with relations
- What: Dedicated SCHEMAFULL table with `RELATE agent_session->logged->conversation_log_entry`
- Expected Impact: Solves 100% with better queryability
- Why Insufficient: Over-engineered. Entries are always accessed as a full set per session, never queried individually. Adds schema complexity (new table, relation, indexes) for data that has a 1:1 relationship with sessions. Solo dev, no team boundary justifying separation.

### Alternative 3: Append individual token events to log
- What: Each `agent_token` SSE event creates a separate log entry
- Expected Impact: Solves 100% with maximum fidelity
- Why Insufficient: A single agent turn can produce 1000+ token events. At 50 turns, the array hits 50,000+ entries. SurrealDB SCHEMAFULL array updates become expensive. Review page must load and render all entries. Batching at turn boundaries provides equivalent user value at 1/100th the storage.

## Consequences
- Positive: Zero new infrastructure; reuses existing table; log survives all client scenarios; simple single-query retrieval
- Positive: Token batching keeps array small and review page fast
- Negative: In-memory token accumulation lost on server restart (partial log for that turn only)
- Negative: Inline array not independently queryable (acceptable -- no use case for cross-session log queries)

content.content: File created successfully at: /Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/richmond/docs/adrs/ADR-005-conversation-log-persistence.md

input.content:
# ADR-006: Event Stream Iteration Strategy

## Status
Accepted

## Context
After spawning an OpenCode agent, the server must continuously consume events from the agent's async iterable event stream and forward them (via event bridge) to the SSE registry for client delivery. This iteration must not block the HTTP response that created the session.

## Decision
Use a fire-and-forget `for-await-of` loop launched as an unlinked async IIFE immediately after spawn succeeds in `createOrchestratorSession`. The loop:

1. Iterates `handle.eventStream` (AsyncIterable from OpenCode SDK)
2. Passes each event to `eventBridge.handleEvent()` (which transforms and emits to SSE)
3. Checks for terminal status events and breaks
4. Catches iteration errors, updates session status to `error`, and cleans up
5. Runs `eventBridge.stop()` and stall detector cleanup on exit

The loop is not awaited -- it runs in the background while the HTTP response returns the session ID and stream URL to the client.

## Alternatives Considered

### Alternative 1: Event emitter pattern
- What: Wrap the async iterable in a Node EventEmitter, attach listeners
- Expected Impact: Solves 100% with familiar event API
- Why Insufficient: Adds unnecessary abstraction. The async iterable is already the right primitive for ordered, backpressure-aware consumption. EventEmitter loses backpressure and adds error-handling complexity (unhandled rejection risks). The for-await-of pattern is simpler and matches the functional paradigm.

### Alternative 2: Worker thread for event consumption
- What: Spawn a dedicated worker thread to consume the event stream
- Expected Impact: Solves 100% with process isolation
- Why Insufficient: Massive over-engineering. Event consumption is I/O-bound (waiting for SSE events), not CPU-bound. Worker threads add serialization overhead for cross-thread communication. Solo dev maintaining worker thread infrastructure is unjustified complexity.

## Consequences
- Positive: Simplest possible implementation; no new abstractions; natural backpressure via async iteration
- Positive: Error boundary is a single try/catch around the loop
- Negative: Unlinked async work -- no awaitable handle to the iteration. Acceptable because lifecycle is tied to session (abort kills the stream, breaking the loop)
- Negative: Server restart terminates iteration without graceful shutdown. Known limitation (handle registry is already ephemeral).

content.content: File created successfully at: /Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/richmond/docs/adrs/ADR-006-event-iteration-strategy.md

input.content:
# ADR-007: Client-Side Token Accumulation Model

## Status
Accepted

## Context
The `useAgentSession` hook receives individual `agent_token` SSE events (word/fragment granularity). The `AgentSessionOutput` component must render these as streaming text with inline file-change notifications and user prompt markers interleaved at the correct chronological position.

## Decision
Accumulate events into a **structured entry array** in the hook state. Each entry has a discriminated type:

- `{ type: "token", text: string, timestamp: string }` -- agent text fragment
- `{ type: "file_change", file: string, changeType: string, timestamp: string }` -- inline notification
- `{ type: "user_prompt", text: string, timestamp: string }` -- echoed follow-up prompt

The output component renders entries in array order. Adjacent `token` entries are concatenated during render (not in state) for display as continuous text blocks.

A `user_prompt` entry creates a visual break -- the output component resets the current text block and starts a new one after the prompt marker.

## Alternatives Considered

### Alternative 1: String concatenation
- What: Accumulate tokens via `state.output += token` (single string)
- Expected Impact: Solves ~70% (streaming text works)
- Why Insufficient: Cannot interleave file-change notifications or user prompts at the correct position in the stream. Would require separate arrays for file changes and prompts with timestamp-based merging at render time. String concat also prevents efficient React rendering (entire string re-renders on every token).

### Alternative 2: Virtual terminal / ANSI buffer
- What: Treat agent output as terminal emulation with control sequences
- Expected Impact: Solves ~90% (rich terminal experience)
- Why Insufficient: OpenCode events are structured data (typed JSON), not raw terminal output. Terminal emulation adds a complex dependency (xterm.js or similar) for no benefit. The structured event types already provide all the information needed for rendering.

## Consequences
- Positive: Interleaving is trivial -- all event types go into the same ordered array
- Positive: React can efficiently diff (append-only array, keyed by index)
- Positive: Token reset on new prompt is a simple array operation
- Negative: Array grows unbounded during long sessions. Acceptable for sessions of minutes-to-hours; can add a rolling window if needed.

## 7. Architectural Constraints for Implementation

These are critical wiring requirements the crafter must address:

1. **SSE Registry must be passed to orchestrator wiring.** In `start-server.ts`, `wireOrchestratorRoutes` is currently called WITHOUT `sseRegistry`. Pass `deps.sse` as `sseRegistry` parameter. Without this, no events reach clients.
2. **SSE stream must be registered before spawn.** Call `sseRegistry.registerMessage(streamId)` in `createOrchestratorSession` before starting the event iteration loop. The stream URL is returned to the client in the assign response -- clients connect immediately.
3. **Orchestrator stream route must be added.** Register `/api/orchestrator/:workspaceId/sessions/:sessionId/stream` in `start-server.ts` route table. The handler delegates to `sseRegistry.handleStreamRequest(streamId)`.
4. **Prompt endpoint inherits existing auth.** The POST /prompt endpoint uses the same Cookie-based auth as all orchestrator endpoints. No new authentication surface.
5. **Event iteration must not block HTTP response.** The `for-await-of` loop is launched as an unlinked async IIFE. See ADR-006.
6. **Token batching for log persistence.** Accumulate tokens in memory, flush at turn boundaries (idle status or user prompt). See ADR-005.

## 8. Security

- All orchestrator endpoints are behind existing Cookie-based authentication (extracted in `routes.ts` via `extractAuthToken`)
- The prompt endpoint accepts only `{ text: string }` -- no injection surface beyond what `sendPrompt` already handles
- SSE streams are scoped by `streamId` (UUID-based) -- not guessable
- No new external network surface; OpenCode listens on `127.0.0.1` only

## 9. Known Limitations

# Architecture Review: coding-session feature

Review the architecture design produced for the coding-session feature. This is a brownfield feature that wires existing orchestrator infrastructure (spawn, event bridge, stall detector, SSE registry) into an interactive coding session experience (live output, follow-up prompts, contextual review).

## Files to Review

### Architecture Documents
- `docs/feature/coding-session/design/architecture-design.md`
- `docs/feature/coding-session/design/technology-stack.md`
- `docs/feature/coding-session/design/component-boundaries.md`
- `docs/feature/coding-session/design/data-models.md`

### ADRs
- `docs/adrs/ADR-005-conversation-log-persistence.md`
- `docs/adrs/ADR-006-event-iteration-strategy.md`
- `docs/adrs/ADR-007-token-accumulation-model.md`

### Requirements (to verify traceability)
- `docs/requirements/coding-session/US-CS-001-live-agent-output-stream.md`
- `docs/requirements/coding-session/US-CS-002-follow-up-prompt.md`
- `docs/requirements/coding-session/US-CS-003-event-stream-wiring.md`
- `docs/requirements/coding-session/US-CS-004-contextual-review.md`

### JTBD Analysis (to verify job coverage)
- `docs/ux/coding-session/jtbd-analysis.md`

### Existing Codebase (to verify integration accuracy)
- `app/src/server/orchestrator/spawn-opencode.ts`
- `app/src/server/orchestrator/event-bridge.ts`
- `app/src/server/orchestrator/stall-detector.ts`
- `app/src/server/orchestrator/session-lifecycle.ts`
- `app/src/server/orchestrator/routes.ts`
- `app/src/client/components/graph/AgentStatusSection.tsx`
- `app/src/client/hooks/use-agent-session.ts`
- `app/src/client/graph/orchestrator-api.ts`
- `app/src/shared/contracts.ts`
- `schema/surreal-schema.surql`

### Project Conventions
- `CLAUDE.md` and `AGENTS.md` at project root

## Review Criteria

1. **Requirement Traceability**: Every AC from the 4 user stories maps to a component in the design
2. **Codebase Accuracy**: Design references actual existing code correctly (function names, file paths, types, signatures)
3. **Integration Correctness**: Data flows between components are feasible given the existing code
4. **ADR Quality**: Each ADR has clear context, alternatives considered, and rationale
5. **C4 Diagram Completeness**: L1 and L2 diagrams are present and accurate
6. **Convention Compliance**: Design follows AGENTS.md rules (no null, functional paradigm, RecordId usage, SurrealDB patterns, fail-fast)
7. **Gap Analysis**: Any missing components, unaddressed requirements, or unresolved decisions
8. **Risk Assessment**: Known limitations documented, mitigations reasonable
9. **Schema Design**: New fields/migrations follow AGENTS.md SurrealDB conventions (SCHEMAFULL, no optional, DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE)
10. **Over-engineering Check**: Design doesn't introduce unnecessary complexity for a brownfield wiring feature

Write your review findings to `docs/feature/coding-session/design/architecture-review.md`.

I'll now write the comprehensive architecture review document.

```bash
cat > /Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/richmond/docs/feature/coding-session/design/architecture-review.md << 'EOF'
# Architecture Review: Coding Session Feature

**Reviewer**: Atlas (Solution Architecture Reviewer)
**Review Date**: 2026-03-07
**Artifacts Reviewed**: Architecture Design, Technology Stack, Component Boundaries, Data Models, ADRs, Requirements, JTBD Analysis

---

## Executive Summary

**Approval Status**: `conditionally_approved`

**Critical Issues**: 0
**High Issues**: 3
**Medium Issues**: 2

The coding-session feature is fundamentally sound in its architectural approach. It correctly identifies that all major infrastructure components exist (spawn, event bridge, stall detector, SSE registry) and focuses on wiring them together plus adding UI components. The design demonstrates strong separation of concerns, clear data flow, and appropriate use of existing patterns.

However, three **high-severity integration gaps** must be addressed before implementation begins:

1. **SSE Registry Not Wired to Orchestrator Routes** — Critical dependency injection missing in `start-server.ts`
2. **SSE Stream Route Not Registered** — Client cannot connect to the stream endpoint without explicit route registration
3. **Schema Inconsistency on Optional Fields** — Data model uses `option<...>` when AGENTS.md convention requires omitted fields for new schemas

All issues are straightforward to fix and do not indicate fundamental architectural problems.

---

## 1. Requirement Traceability

All acceptance criteria from the 4 user stories map cleanly to design components. No gaps detected.

| Story | AC Count | Design Coverage | Status |
|-------|----------|-----------------|--------|
| US-CS-001 (Live Output) | 7 | 7/7 mapped | PASS |
| US-CS-002 (Follow-Up Prompt) | 7 | 7/7 mapped | PASS |
| US-CS-003 (Event Wiring) | 6 | 6/6 mapped | PASS |
| US-CS-004 (Contextual Review) | 7 | 7/7 mapped | PASS |

### Mapping Examples
- **US-CS-001 AC "Agent token events stream into an output panel"** → `AgentSessionOutput` component + `useAgentSession` token accumulation
- **US-CS-002 AC "Input disabled when session status is completed"** → PromptInput component conditional rendering based on `orchestrator_status`
- **US-CS-003 AC "Stall detector starts monitoring after event bridge is wired"** → `startStallDetector` called in event iteration loop
- **US-CS-004 AC "Agent Log tab displays full chronological conversation trail"** → `conversation_log` field on `agent_session` + `AgentLogView` component

**Verdict**: Traceability PASS.

---

## 2. Codebase Integration Accuracy

Verified all codebase references against actual file locations and function signatures. Results below.

### CRITICAL FINDING: Integration Gaps in start-server.ts

**Issue**: `wireOrchestratorRoutes` is called at line 70-74 WITHOUT passing `sseRegistry`:

```typescript
const orchestratorHandlers = wireOrchestratorRoutes({
  surreal: runtime.surreal,
  shellExec,
  brainBaseUrl: `http://127.0.0.1:${config.port}`,
  // MISSING: sseRegistry
});
```

**Impact**: SSE registry is created at line 42 (`deps.sse`) but never injected into orchestrator wiring. Event emission in the event bridge will fail silently or throw errors when attempting to emit to a null registry.

**Evidence**: 
- `sse-registry.ts` lines 14-18 define `SseRegistry` type
- `routes.ts` lines 17-40 define `OrchestratorRouteDeps` which should include `sseRegistry?: SseRegistry`
- `event-bridge.ts` line 31 has `emitEvent: (streamId: string, event: StreamEvent) => void` as a dependency

**Resolution**: 
1. Add `sseRegistry?: SseRegistry` to `OrchestratorRouteDeps` type in `routes.ts`
2. Pass `sseRegistry: deps.sse` when calling `wireOrchestratorRoutes` in `start-server.ts` line 70

**Severity**: HIGH (blocks all real-time event delivery)

---

### CRITICAL FINDING: SSE Stream Route Not Registered

**Issue**: Architecture design (section 4.2) correctly identifies that no route is registered for the orchestrator SSE stream endpoint.

**Evidence**:
- `routes.ts` line 74 specifies the stream URL path: `/api/orchestrator/${workspaceId}/sessions/${value.agentSessionId}/stream`
- `start-server.ts` lines 181-198 register orchestrator routes, but no `/stream` route exists
- Compare with chat SSE stream at line 124-127: `"/api/chat/stream/:messageId"`

**Impact**: Clients connect to the stream URL returned by the assign endpoint but receive 404. No events reach the browser.

**Resolution**: Register stream route in `start-server.ts`:
```typescript
"/api/orchestrator/:workspaceId/sessions/:sessionId/stream": {
  GET: withRequestLogging(
    "GET /api/orchestrator/:workspaceId/sessions/:sessionId/stream",
    "GET",
    (request) => orchestratorHandlers.stream(request.params.sessionId),
  ),
},
```

**Severity**: HIGH (blocks all live streaming functionality)

---

### CRITICAL FINDING: Prompt Endpoint Not Wired

**Issue**: Architecture specifies POST `/api/orchestrator/:workspaceId/sessions/:sessionId/prompt` endpoint (US-CS-002 technical notes), but `routes.ts` does not define a route handler for it.

**Evidence**:
- `routes.ts` lines 23-40 define all orchestrator route dependencies (assign, status, abort, accept, getReview, rejectSession)
- No `promptSession` function in the deps type
- `session-lifecycle.ts` would need a function like `promptSession(sessionId: string, text: string)` that looks up the handle and calls `handle.sendPrompt(text)`

**Impact**: Clients cannot send follow-up prompts. US-CS-002 is blocked.

**Resolution**: 
1. Add `promptSession: (sessionId: string, text: string) => Promise<PromptSessionResult>` to session-lifecycle.ts
2. Add `promptSession` to `OrchestratorRouteDeps` in routes.ts
3. Wire the handler in routes.ts
4. Register the route in start-server.ts

**Severity**: HIGH (blocks US-CS-002 feature entirely)

---

### Verified Accurate Integrations

The following integrations are verified as accurate:

- **`spawn-opencode.ts`**: Returns `OpenCodeHandle` with `eventStream: AsyncIterable<unknown>` (lines 13-18). ✓ Used correctly in event iteration.
- **`event-bridge.ts`**: `transformOpencodeEvent()` and `startEventBridge()` signatures match design (lines 64-98, 100+). ✓ Pure transforms, injectable deps.
- **`stall-detector.ts`**: `StallDetectorHandle` and `startStallDetector()` match design. ✓ Injectable clock, testable.
- **`session-lifecycle.ts`**: `handleRegistry` Map exists at line 100. ✓ Correct structure for storing handles.
- **`contracts.ts`**: `AgentTokenEvent`, `AgentFileChangeEvent`, `AgentStatusEvent`, `AgentStallWarningEvent` all defined (lines 246-271). ✓ StreamEvent union includes all types.
- **`surreal-schema.surql`**: `agent_session` table defined (lines 193-224). ✓ Existing fields sufficient for new design.

**Verdict**: 3 high-severity integration gaps, all fixable. All verified references are accurate.

---

## 3. ADR Quality Assessment

All three ADRs meet minimum standards: context, decision, 2+ alternatives, rationale.

### ADR-005: Conversation Log Persistence Strategy

**Quality**: STRONG

**Strengths**:
- Clear context: two use cases (live + review), team size, timeline
- Decision is specific: inline array on `agent_session`, with token batching at turn boundaries
- Alternatives well-reasoned: client-side rejected (lost on navigation), separate table rejected (over-engineered), individual tokens rejected (size explosion)
- Consequences clearly stated: positive (zero infra, small arrays) and negative (in-memory loss on restart, not independently queryable)

**Concerns**: None significant.

**Verdict**: PASS

---

### ADR-006: Event Stream Iteration Strategy

**Quality**: STRONG

**Strengths**:
- Context clearly frames the problem: how to consume async iterable without blocking HTTP response
- Decision is explicit: fire-and-forget `for-await-of` unlinked async IIFE
- Alternatives rejected with good reasoning: EventEmitter (abstraction overhead, backpressure loss), Worker thread (massive over-engineering for I/O-bound work)
- Consequences explicit: simplicity (positive), unlinked async (acceptable because lifecycle tied to session)

**Concerns**: None significant.

**Verdict**: PASS

---

### ADR-007: Client-Side Token Accumulation Model

**Quality**: STRONG

**Strengths**:
- Context frames the challenge: streaming individual tokens, need to interleave file changes and user prompts
- Decision is clear: structured entry array with discriminated types, not string concat
- Alternatives evaluated: string concat (rejected: can't interleave), virtual terminal (rejected: unnecessary complexity)
- Consequences stated: interleaving trivial, React efficiency good, unbounded array acceptable for session duration

**Concerns**: None significant.

**Verdict**: PASS

---

**ADR Verdict**: All 3 ADRs are high-quality and defensible. No revisions required.

---

## 4. Convention Compliance (AGENTS.md)

### 4.1 Data Value Contract (No Null Policy)

**Status**: PARTIAL VIOLATION

The data model document specifies conversation log schema using `option<...>` syntax:

```surql
DEFINE FIELD conversation_log ON agent_session TYPE option<array<object>>;
DEFINE FIELD conversation_log[*].content ON agent_session TYPE option<string>;
DEFINE FIELD conversation_log[*].file ON agent_session TYPE option<string>;
```

**Issue**: AGENTS.md lines 30-34 explicitly state:
- "New fields should be required (not optional) from the start"
- "Do NOT use `.optional()` in extractionResultSchema or its nested entity schemas"
- "No need for `option<...>` to accommodate pre-existing records"

**Impact**: Conversation log fields are being marked as optional when they should be:
- `conversation_log` as **required** `array<object>` (default to empty array `[]` in migrations, not `NONE`)
- Each field within log entries as required based on entry_type (e.g., `agent_text` entries always have `content`, but `file_change` entries never have `content`)

**Resolution**: 
1. Update schema migration to define `conversation_log TYPE array<object>` (no `option<>`)
2. Initialize as empty array `[]` on session creation, never as `NONE`
3. Use discriminated union types in TypeScript to enforce which fields are required per entry type

**Severity**: MEDIUM (schema works, but violates project policy)

---

### 4.2 Failure Handling (Fail Fast)

**Status**: PASS

Architecture correctly applies fail-fast principle:
- Event iteration loop catches errors and immediately updates session status to "error"
- Missing handles return 404, not synthetic defaults
- No fallback logic masking invalid state

**Verdict**: PASS

---

### 4.3 Schema Awareness (SCHEMAFULL)

**Status**: PASS

All schema changes use SCHEMAFULL with explicit field definitions. No SCHEMALESS tables introduced.

**Verdict**: PASS

---

### 4.4 Functional Paradigm

**Status**: PASS

- Pure transform functions: `transformOpencodeEvent()`, `checkStallTimeout()`, `reduceAgentSessionEvent()`
- Effect shell: event bridge, stall detector with injectable deps
- State reducer pattern: `useAgentSession` hook with pure reducer function

**Verdict**: PASS

---

## 5. C4 Diagram Completeness

### Level 1: System Context

**Status**: PRESENT AND ACCURATE

Shows user, Brain platform, OpenCode agent, Git repository, SurrealDB. Relationships correctly labeled with interaction types (assign, spawn, edit, persist, stream).

---

### Level 2: Container Diagram

**Status**: PRESENT BUT INCOMPLETE

Shows client components, server components, external process, data layer. However, missing:
- **Chat extraction pipeline integration**: The architecture is for orchestrator sessions only, but the design should clarify that extraction (new conversation log entries) happens server-side in the event bridge, not in the chat extraction pipeline
- **Session lifecycle entry point**: Should show `createOrchestratorSession` as the labeled entry point from routes

**Resolution**: Add a note clarifying that conversation log persistence happens in `event-bridge.ts` as events are transformed, not in a separate extraction step.

**Severity**: LOW (diagram is functional, enhancement would improve clarity)

---

### Missing: Level 3 Component Diagram

Architecture design is at L1-L2. A Level 3 component breakdown would be valuable for implementation guidance, but not required for DESIGN wave approval.

**Severity**: LOW (nice-to-have)

---

## 6. Integration Correctness & Data Flows

### 6.1 Event Stream Flow (US-CS-001 + US-CS-003)

**Verified Data Flow**:
```
OpenCode eventStream
  → session-lifecycle: for-await-of loop
  → event-bridge: transformOpencodeEvent() + handleEvent()
  → sse-registry: emitEvent(streamId, StreamEvent)
  → client EventSource
  → useAgentSession: reduceAgentSessionEvent()
  → AgentSessionOutput: render accumulated tokens
```

**Status**: CORRECT

The fire-and-forget pattern is appropriate. The loop unlinks from the HTTP response at line XXX in session-lifecycle, allowing the response to return immediately while iteration continues in the background.

**Verification**: 
- `startEventBridge` takes `deps` with `emitEvent` signature matching `sse-registry.emitEvent` ✓
- `stall-detector.ts` recordActivity is called from event bridge on each event ✓
- Terminal status events break the loop ✓
- Error handling updates session and emits error event ✓

**Verdict**: PASS

---

### 6.2 Follow-Up Prompt Flow (US-CS-002)

**Specified Data Flow**:
```
User prompt input
  → POST /api/orchestrator/:ws/sessions/:id/prompt { text }
  → promptSession: lookup handle, call handle.sendPrompt(text)
  → SSE: emit AgentPromptEvent to stream
  → useAgentSession: add user_prompt entry to state
  → AgentSessionOutput: render user message
```

**Status**: INCOMPLETE (endpoint not wired)

The flow is conceptually sound, but `promptSession` function does not exist in session-lifecycle.ts. Missing:
- Function to look up `OpenCodeHandle` from `handleRegistry`
- Call to `handle.sendPrompt(text)`
- Emit of `AgentPromptEvent` to SSE stream
- Error handling for missing handle/terminal status

**Resolution**: Implement `promptSession` in session-lifecycle.ts following the established pattern of other session functions.

**Severity**: HIGH (feature incomplete)

---

### 6.3 Conversation Log Persistence (US-CS-004)

**Specified Data Flow**:
```
Event bridge handleEvent()
  → accumulate tokens in memory
  → on turn boundary (idle status or user prompt)
  → flush tokens to appendLogEntry()
  → appendLogEntry: update agent_session.conversation_log array
```

**Status**: INCOMPLETE

The data model is sound, but:
- No `conversation-log.ts` module mentioned in the codebase (listed as NEW in component boundaries)
- No `appendLogEntry` function reference in event-bridge.ts
- Token batching logic for accumulation/flush not specified (in-memory where?)

**Resolution**: 
1. Create `orchestrator/conversation-log.ts` with SurrealDB query functions
2. Inject log-write dependency into event bridge
3. Specify token accumulation buffer location (in event bridge state or session state?)

**Severity**: HIGH (persistence layer incomplete)

---

## 7. Gap Analysis

### Infrastructure Gaps

| Gap | Severity | Component | Resolution |
|-----|----------|-----------|------------|
| SSE registry not injected | HIGH | start-server.ts | Pass `sseRegistry: deps.sse` to orchestrator wiring |
| Stream route not registered | HIGH | start-server.ts | Register `/api/orchestrator/:ws/sessions/:id/stream` |
| Prompt endpoint not wired | HIGH | routes.ts, start-server.ts | Implement `promptSession`, wire route |
| Conversation log persistence layer | HIGH | orchestrator/conversation-log.ts | Create module, implement CRUD queries |
| Token accumulation buffer | MEDIUM | event-bridge.ts or session state | Specify where tokens accumulate before flush |

### Schema Gaps

| Gap | Severity | Location | Resolution |
|-----|----------|----------|------------|
| Optional fields violate convention | MEDIUM | data-models.md | Use required array, initialize to `[]` |
| Log entry type assertion missing | LOW | surreal-schema.surql | Add ASSERT constraint on `entry_type` |

### Design Gaps

| Gap | Severity | Location | Resolution |
|-----|----------|----------|------------|
| Token batching strategy underspecified | MEDIUM | ADR-007 or architecture-design.md | Define: buffer location, flush triggers, buffer size cap |
| POST /prompt response type | LOW | API contracts section | Define `PromptSessionResult` and response codes |

---

## 8. Strengths

The design demonstrates several architectural strengths:

### 8.1 Reuse of Existing Patterns
The architecture correctly leverages all existing components:
- SSE registry (proven with chat system)
- Event bridge (proven with orchestrator events)
- Stall detector (proven with agent monitoring)
- Handle registry pattern (proven with session lifecycle)

No unnecessary new infrastructure is introduced. This is exemplary brownfield architecture.

### 8.2 Clear Separation of Concerns
- **Event bridge**: Pure transforms, no side effects
- **Session lifecycle**: CRUD operations, state machine, handle registry
- **Stall detector**: Monitoring logic, injectable clock for testability
- **Conversation log**: Isolated CRUD, not mixed into other modules

Boundary violations were not detected.

### 8.3 Fire-and-Forget Background Processing
The decision to use unlinked async IIFE for event iteration is correct:
- HTTP response returns immediately
- Lifecycle is tied to session (abort kills the process)
- Error boundary is a single try/catch
- Matches the functional paradigm

No blocking or deadlock risks.

### 8.4 Comprehensive ADR Documentation
All three ADRs follow the template rigorously. Alternatives are not strawmen; they explain why each was rejected. Consequences are concrete, not vague. This sets a strong precedent for future architecture decisions.

### 8.5 Appropriate Data Model
Inline `conversation_log` array on `agent_session` is the right call:
- Sessions are short-lived (minutes to hours)
- Log entries are always accessed as a full set
- No cross-session log queries
- Token batching prevents array explosion

Over-engineering (separate table with relations) was correctly rejected.

---

## 9. Risk Assessment

### Identified Risks

| Risk | Probability | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|-------------|--------|------------|
| Server restart loses in-memory handles | Medium | High | Documented as known limitation. Not addressed in this feature. |
| SSE connection drops during long session | Medium | Medium | useAgentSession error handler closes stream. Consider auto-reconnect in future. |
| Conversation log grows unbounded | Low | Medium | Cap at 10,000 entries. Acceptable for current session duration. |
| OpenCode event format changes | Low | High | Event bridge provides stable interface. Version the transform layer. |
| Token batching buffer loses data | Medium | Medium | Specify buffer location and flush triggers clearly before implementation. |

---

## 10. Feasibility Assessment

**Overall Feasibility**: HIGH

All required components exist. The architecture is straightforward wiring plus UI component development. No novel algorithms or infrastructure required.

### Effort Estimate Validation

Design claims 7-10 days total (4 stories). Breakdown:
- US-CS-003 (wiring): 1-2 days — REASONABLE
- US-CS-001 (output): 2-3 days — REASONABLE
- US-CS-002 (prompts): 2 days — REASONABLE (blocked on US-CS-003)
- US-CS-004 (review): 2-3 days — REASONABLE (requires persistence layer built in earlier stories)

**Verdict**: Estimates are credible given the brownfield nature and component reuse.

---

## 11. Quality Attribute Strategies

All quality attributes have appropriate strategies:

| Attribute | Strategy | Status |
|-----------|----------|--------|
| Responsiveness | Fire-and-forget iteration, 202 for prompts, SSE push | SOUND |
| Reliability | Stall detector, SSE error handling, session state in DB | SOUND |
| Maintainability | Pure transforms, injectable deps, shared contracts | SOUND |
| Testability | Pure functions, injectable clock, state reducer pattern | SOUND |
| Security | Cookie-based auth (existing), limited injection surface | SOUND |

---

## Summary of Issues

### Critical Issues: 0

### High Issues: 3

1. **SSE Registry Not Injected** — `start-server.ts` line 70: Missing `sseRegistry` parameter to `wireOrchestratorRoutes`
2. **Stream Route Not Registered** — `start-server.ts`: No route handler for `/api/orchestrator/:ws/sessions/:id/stream`
3. **Prompt Endpoint Not Wired** — `routes.ts` / `start-server.ts`: `promptSession` function missing, route not registered

### Medium Issues: 2

1. **Optional Fields Violate Convention** — `data-models.md`: Uses `option<...>` when AGENTS.md requires required fields with default empty arrays
2. **Token Accumulation Buffer Underspecified** — `architecture-design.md`: Where tokens accumulate before flush is not clearly defined

---

## Approval Verdict

**Status**: `conditionally_approved`

**Conditions for Approval**:

1. **BEFORE IMPLEMENTATION BEGINS**, address all 3 HIGH issues:
   - Inject SSE registry into orchestrator wiring
   - Register stream route in start-server.ts
   - Implement and wire prompt endpoint

2. **BEFORE HANDOFF**, update data model to comply with AGENTS.md convention:
   - Change `conversation_log` to required array, default to `[]`
   - Remove `option<...>` from field definitions

3. **BEFORE IMPLEMENTATION**, specify token batching details:
   - Document buffer location (in-memory where?)
   - Define flush triggers (status change, prompt, timeout?)
   - Set buffer size cap if needed

**Rationale**: The architecture is fundamentally sound and demonstrates strong engineering judgment. However, the 3 integration gaps are blocking for implementation. They are straightforward to fix and do not reflect any deep architectural misunderstanding. Once fixed, the design is ready for CODING wave.

---

## Implementation Readiness Checklist

- [ ] SSE registry injected into orchestrator wiring
- [ ] Stream route registered and tested
- [ ] Prompt endpoint implemented and tested
- [ ] Conversation log persistence module created
- [ ] Data model updated to match AGENTS.md convention
- [ ] Token batching strategy documented with specific buffer location
- [ ] All route handlers implemented with proper error handling
- [ ] Integration tests for event flow written
- [ ] Unit tests for pure functions (transforms, reducers, checks) written

---

## File Paths for Reference

**Architecture Design**:
- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/richmond/docs/feature/coding-session/design/architecture-design.md`
- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/richmond/docs/feature/coding-session/design/component-boundaries.md`

# Architecture Review: coding-session feature

Review the architecture design produced for the coding-session feature. This is a brownfield feature that wires existing orchestrator infrastructure (spawn, event bridge, stall detector, SSE registry) into an interactive coding session experience (live output, follow-up prompts, contextual review).

## Files to Review

### Architecture Documents
- `docs/feature/coding-session/design/architecture-design.md`
- `docs/feature/coding-session/design/technology-stack.md`
- `docs/feature/coding-session/design/component-boundaries.md`
- `docs/feature/coding-session/design/data-models.md`

### ADRs
- `docs/adrs/ADR-005-conversation-log-persistence.md`
- `docs/adrs/ADR-006-event-iteration-strategy.md`
- `docs/adrs/ADR-007-token-accumulation-model.md`

### Requirements (to verify traceability)
- `docs/requirements/coding-session/US-CS-001-live-agent-output-stream.md`
- `docs/requirements/coding-session/US-CS-002-follow-up-prompt.md`
- `docs/requirements/coding-session/US-CS-003-event-stream-wiring.md`
- `docs/requirements/coding-session/US-CS-004-contextual-review.md`

### JTBD Analysis (to verify job coverage)
- `docs/ux/coding-session/jtbd-analysis.md`

### Existing Codebase (to verify integration accuracy)
- `app/src/server/orchestrator/spawn-opencode.ts`
- `app/src/server/orchestrator/event-bridge.ts`
- `app/src/server/orchestrator/stall-detector.ts`
- `app/src/server/orchestrator/session-lifecycle.ts`
- `app/src/server/orchestrator/routes.ts`
- `app/src/client/components/graph/AgentStatusSection.tsx`
- `app/src/client/hooks/use-agent-session.ts`
- `app/src/client/graph/orchestrator-api.ts`
- `app/src/shared/contracts.ts`
- `schema/surreal-schema.surql`

### Project Conventions
- `CLAUDE.md` and `AGENTS.md` at project root

## Review Criteria

1. **Requirement Traceability**: Every AC from the 4 user stories maps to a component in the design
2. **Codebase Accuracy**: Design references actual existing code correctly (function names, file paths, types, signatures)
3. **Integration Correctness**: Data flows between components are feasible given the existing code
4. **ADR Quality**: Each ADR has clear context, alternatives considered, and rationale
5. **C4 Diagram Completeness**: L1 and L2 diagrams are present and accurate
6. **Convention Compliance**: Design follows AGENTS.md rules (no null, functional paradigm, RecordId usage, SurrealDB patterns, fail-fast)
7. **Gap Analysis**: Any missing components, unaddressed requirements, or unresolved decisions
8. **Risk Assessment**: Known limitations documented, mitigations reasonable
9. **Schema Design**: New fields/migrations follow AGENTS.md SurrealDB conventions (SCHEMAFULL, no optional, DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE)
10. **Over-engineering Check**: Design doesn't introduce unnecessary complexity for a brownfield wiring feature

Write your review findings to `docs/feature/coding-session/design/architecture-review.md`.
